title: Cell counting + viability (trypan blue)
description: Universal routine: 1:1 with 0.4% trypan blue, hemocytometer count, viability %. Source: Gibco Cell Culture Basics.

payload.description: Universal cell counting and viability by trypan blue exclusion (Gibco Cell Culture Basics). Mix 1:1 (10 uL cell suspension + 10 uL 0.4% trypan blue), a 2-fold dilution. Load 10 uL onto a hemocytometer and examine immediately (dead cells take up trypan blue over time). Count viable (unstained, refractile) cells in the central 1 mm^2 square in a zig-zag; dead cells stain blue. cells/mL = (viable cells in the central square) x dilution factor x 10^4. Viability % = [1 - (blue cells / total cells)] x 100; healthy log-phase cultures should be at least 95% viable. Automated alternative: the Countess counter uses the same 1:1 trypan blue mix. Sources: Gibco trypan blue exclusion https://www.thermofisher.com/us/en/home/references/gibco-cell-culture-basics/cell-culture-protocols/trypan-blue-exclusion.html ; Gibco hemocytometer https://www.thermofisher.com/us/en/home/references/gibco-cell-culture-basics/cell-culture-protocols/counting-cells-in-a-hemacytometer.html

cell_line.name: Any cell line
cell_line.notes: Count promptly after mixing with trypan blue. Aim for at least 95% viability in healthy cultures. Source: Gibco (see description).

planned_events.notes: Mix 10 uL cell suspension + 10 uL 0.4% trypan blue (1:1, a 2-fold dilution). Load 10 uL on a hemocytometer and examine immediately. Count viable (unstained) cells in the central 1 mm^2 square in a zig-zag. cells/mL = count x dilution factor x 10^4. Viability % = [1 - (blue / total)] x 100. Aim for at least 95% in healthy log-phase cultures. The Countess automated counter uses the same 1:1 mix.
